[Stakeholder Support for Regulatory Harmonization and Expanded Nuclear Power: Outcomes of HPS/NCRP Open Forums.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41532850/)

evidence.claim:
PubMed ID: 8056600. 1994/09/01 00:00. this study was to make an intercomparison of the results, by taking the IAEA 
protocol as a standard. The largest deviation contributed by any of these 
protocols
evidence.raw_snippet:
PubMed ID: 8056600. 1994/09/01 00:00. this study was to make an intercomparison of the results, by taking the IAEA 
protocol as a standard. The largest deviation contributed by any of these 
protocols was recorded for each quality. It was found that AAPM, NCRP and ICRU 
protocols contributed 0.94% for 1.25 MeV gamma ray, NACP contributed 2.12% for 
the 6 MV x-rays, and NACP contributed 2.35% for 10 MV x-rays. Since the 
acceptable limit of deviation set by the IAEA for this absorbed dose work is
